title: lasiosiphon passargei
side_effects: Lasiosiphon passargei can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness due to its toxic compounds. Ingestion of the plant's roots or leaves can also lead to allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can cause liver damage and kidney problems, as well as an increased risk of seizures and coma. Improper use of the plant can also interact with other medications, exacerbating pre-existing health conditions and potentially leading to life-threatening complications.
